questions.question: Which attribute on the <video> tag displays a thumbnail image before the video plays?
questions.explanation: The poster attribute specifies an image URL to display as a placeholder before the user plays the video.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the <source> element inside an <audio> or <video> tag?
choices: It defines the volume level of the media
It provides fallback text for unsupported browsers
It specifies multiple media files so the browser can choose a supported format
It adds subtitles to the media
questions.explanation: The <source> element lets you list multiple media files in different formats (e.g., mp3, ogg). The browser picks the first format it supports.

questions.question: Which attribute causes a video to start playing automatically when the page loads?
questions.explanation: The autoplay attribute tells the browser to begin playing the media as soon as enough data has loaded. Many browsers now block autoplay unless the video is also muted.

questions.question: Which value for the preload attribute tells the browser not to preload any media data?
questions.explanation: Setting preload="none" instructs the browser not to download any part of the media file until the user initiates playback. The value "metadata" loads only metadata, and "auto" lets the browser decide.

questions.question: The ___ attribute on a media element causes the audio or video to restart from the beginning after it finishes playing.
questions.explanation: The loop attribute is a boolean attribute that makes the media replay continuously from the start each time it ends.

questions.question: The ___ attribute on a media element shows the browser's built-in play, pause, and volume buttons.
questions.explanation: Adding the controls attribute displays the browser's default media playback controls, allowing users to play, pause, adjust volume, and seek.
